setting.note: Pi Patel grows up in the French-colonial town of Pondicherry, on India's south-eastern coast, where his father keeps a zoo set among the alleys and beds of the old Botanical Garden. When the family decides to emigrate they sail from Madras, and the story is later told to a visitor in Canada. The map anchors the Indian childhood: the garden, the White Town quarter and the port of departure.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Yann Martel, first published 2001. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Life of Pi, Yann Martel (2001). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The burnt orange of the tiger, the lifeboat and the whistle that runs through the book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
